name: Cal-Ed
description_short: Cal-Ed Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution in California, PA offering savings, checking, loans, and credit cards to its members.
description_long:
Cal-Ed Federal Credit Union operates as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution serving the California, Pennsylvania area. The organization's stated mission is "Not for profit, Not for Charity, But for Service," reflecting its cooperative structure where members own and control the institution rather than external shareholders. As a federal credit union, Cal-Ed is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and maintains insurance protection for member deposits.

Cal-Ed offers a comprehensive range of financial services including savings accounts, checking accounts, Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), various loan products, certificates (share certificates), credit cards, and debit cards. The credit union operates from a location in the California Technology Park and provides member convenience through two drive-through teller stations. Members can access services through multiple channels including online banking and direct phone support.

The institution distinguishes itself through its commitment to personal service and member accessibility. Operating during traditional business hours (Monday-Friday, 9 AM to 4 PM), Cal-Ed maintains direct telephone support via 724-938-3280 or 1-800-GO-CALED. The physical location with drive-through facilities and the focus on service-oriented operations reflect a community-based approach typical of smaller credit unions. The website emphasizes special offers and member services.

As a credit union, Cal-Ed serves a defined membership base and operates with the structural advantages of member ownership, including potential for better rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks. However, membership eligibility requirements typically apply, and the organization's smaller size may limit the breadth of specialized services compared to larger financial institutions. The website provides basic service information but limited detail regarding specific product terms, rates, or membership requirements.
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure with NCUA insurance protection
Offers comprehensive financial products including savings, checking, IRAs, loans, and credit cards in one institution
Two drive-through teller stations for convenient service access
Local, community-based credit union with direct personal support via phone (724-938-3280 or 1-800-GO-CALED)
Online banking available for member convenience
Dedicated support lines for lost/stolen cards (credit card: 1-800-449-7728; debit card: 1-833-337-6075)
Promotes special offers and member benefits
cons: Limited service hours (Monday-Friday 9 AM to 4 PM only, closed weekends)
Membership eligibility requirements likely apply, restricting who can join
Website provides minimal information about interest rates, fees, loan terms, or specific product details
Geographic location in California, PA may limit physical accessibility for non-local members
No mention of mobile app or advanced digital banking features beyond basic online banking
best_for: Community members in California, Pennsylvania area seeking local financial institution relationship
Members prioritizing not-for-profit, member-owned financial structure with cooperative values
Individuals seeking comprehensive financial services (savings, checking, loans, credit cards) from single institution
Consumers valuing personal service and direct phone support over digital-first banking
diagnosis: Cal-Ed Federal Credit Union is best for California, PA area residents and eligible members seeking a member-owned, full-service financial institution with personal service orientation. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility requirements apply, service hours are limited to business hours only, and detailed product information is not readily available on their website, requiring direct contact for rates and terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Cal-Ed — Credit Union in CALIFORNIA, PA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Cal-Ed — NCUA-insured credit union in CALIFORNIA, PA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
